channel.title: 2026-05-06 AI × Science 文献日报 RSS
channel.description: 2026-05-06 当天筛选出的 AI × 物理 / 化学 / 材料交叉文献 RSS。

item.title: Predicting Euler Characteristics and Constructing Topological Structure Using Machine Learning Techniques
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Gyunghun Yu (Department of Physics, Kyung Hee University, Seoul, South Korea), Seong Min Park (Department of Physics, Kyung Hee University, Seoul, South Korea), Han Gyu Yoon (Department of Physics, Kyung Hee University, Seoul, South Korea), Tae Jung Moon (Department of Physics, Kyung Hee University, Seoul, South Korea), Jun Woo Choi (Center for Spintronics, Korea Institute of Science and Technology, Seoul, South Korea), Hee Young Kwon (Center for Spintronics, Korea Institute of Science and Technology, Seoul, South Korea), Changyeon Won (Department of Physics, Kyung Hee University, Seoul, South Korea)
arXiv:2605.02947v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: This study proposes a novel approach to extract topological properties, specifically the Euler characteristic, from input images using neural networks without relying on large pre-existing datasets but with a single geometric image. Inspired by solid-state physics, where topological properties of magnetic structures are derived from spin field analysis, our model generates a unit vector field from an image, interpreted as a spin configuration. The Euler characteristic is then predicted by computing the skyrmion number of this generated spin configuration. Remarkably, the network learns to construct chiral magnetic textures without access to ground-truth chiral spin configurations, relying instead on only a single, simple geometric image an…

item.title: 量子几何偶极：平带中味铁磁性的拓扑增强机制
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Lett.
👤 Lei Chen, Sayed Ali Akbar Ghorashi, Jennifer Cano, and Valentin Crépel
该研究指出，平带莫尔材料中鲁棒的味极化相源于一种鲜被关注的量子几何量——量子几何偶极。这一发现为理解平带体系中自发极化的微观起源提供了新的拓扑视角，拓展了量子几何在关联电子现象中的作用。

item.title: Graph Neural Networks in the Wilson Loop Representation of Abelian Lattice Gauge Theories
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Ali Rayat, Gia-Wei Chern
arXiv:2605.03901v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Local gauge structures play a central role in a wide range of condensed matter systems and synthetic quantum platforms, where they emerge as effective descriptions of strongly correlated phases and engineered dynamics. We introduce a gauge-invariant graph neural network (GNN) architecture for Abelian lattice gauge models, in which symmetry is enforced explicitly through local gauge-invariant inputs, such as Wilson loops, and preserved throughout message passing, eliminating redundant gauge degrees of freedom while retaining expressive power. We benchmark the approach on both Z₂ and U(1) lattice gauge models, achieving accurate predictions of global observables and spatially resolved quantities despite the nonlocal correlations induced by gau…

item.title: Conservative quantum offline model-based optimization
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Kristian Sotirov, Annie E. Paine, Savvas Varsamopoulos, Antonio A. Gentile, Osvaldo Simeone
arXiv:2506.19714v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: Offline model-based optimization (MBO) refers to the task of optimizing a black-box objective function using only a fixed set of prior input-output data, without any active experimentation. Recent work has introduced quantum extremal learning (QEL), which leverages the expressive power of variational quantum circuits to learn accurate surrogate functions by training on a few data points. However, as widely studied in the classical machine learning literature, predictive models may incorrectly extrapolate objective values in unexplored regions, leading to the selection of overly optimistic solutions. In this paper, we propose integrating QEL with conservative objective models (COM) - a regularization technique aimed at ensuring caut…

item.title: 从示例学习正确行为：自主智能体顺序执行验证
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Reshabh K Sharma, Gaurav Mittal, Yu Hu
提出一种仅需2-10条通过执行轨迹即可自动学习正确行为并验证新执行的算法。结合编译理论中的支配节点分析和多模态大语言模型的语义理解，构建基于前缀树接收器的广义真值模型，通过拓扑子序列匹配验证执行。受控实验表明仅3条训练轨迹即可高精度检测产品缺陷和虚假成功，适用于UI测试、代码生成和机器人流程等多领域。

item.title: Magic-Informed Quantum Architecture Search
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Vincenzo Lipardi, Domenica Dibenedetto, Georgios Stamoulis, Mark H. M. Winands
arXiv:2605.03932v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Nonstabilizerness, commonly referred to as magic, is a fundamental resource underpinning quantum advantage. In this paper, we propose a magic-informed quantum architecture search (QAS) technique that enables control over a quantum resource within the general framework of circuit design. Inspired by the AlphaGo approach, we tackle the problem with a Monte Carlo Tree Search technique equipped with a Graph Neural Network (GNN) that estimates the magic of candidate quantum circuits. The GNN model induces a magic-based bias that steers the search toward either high- or low-magic regimes, depending on the target objective. We benchmark the proposed magic-informed QAS technique on both the structured ground-state energy problem and on the more ge…

item.title: Pretrained Model Representations as Acquisition Signals for Active Learning of MLIPs
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Eszter Varga-Umbrich, Shikha Surana, Paul Duckworth, Jules Tilly, Olivier Peltre, Zachary Weller-Davies
arXiv:2605.03964v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Training machine learning interatomic potentials (MLIPs) for reactive chemistry is often bottlenecked by the high cost of quantum chemical labels and the scarcity of transition state configurations in candidate pools. Active learning (AL) can mitigate these costs, but its effectiveness hinges on the acquisition rule. We investigate whether the latent space of a pretrained MLIP already contains the information necessary for effective acquisition, eliminating the need for auxiliary uncertainty heads, Bayesian training and fine-tuning, or committee ensembles. We introduce two acquisition signals derived directly from a pretrained MACE potential: a finite-width neural tangent kernel (NTK) and an activation kernel built from hidden latent space…

item.title: Quantum Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning via Variational Quantum Circuits
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yu-Ting Lee, Samuel Yen-Chi Chen, Fu-Chieh Chang
arXiv:2605.03434v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement learning is one of the most challenging learning paradigms where efficacy and efficiency gains are extremely valuable. Hierarchical reinforcement learning is a variant that leverages temporal abstraction to structure decision-making. While parametrized quantum computations have shown success in non-hierarchical reinforcement learning, whether these advantages adapt to hierarchical decision-making remains a critical open question. In this work, we develop a hybrid hierarchical agent based on the option-critic architecture. This hybrid agent substitutes classical components with variational quantum circuits for feature extractors, option-value functions, termination functions, and intra-option policies. Evaluated on standard benc…

item.title: Hybrid Variational Quantum-Classical Neural Networks for CPT-Based Prediction of Shear Wave Velocity and Soil Behavior Type Index: A Sanity Check Study with Ensemble and Deep Learning Methods
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Omer Muhie Eldeen Taha, Sarah Mustafa Ahmed
This research introduces the inaugural utilization of a Hybrid Variational Quantum-Classical Neural Network (VQNN) for geotechnical site characterization utilizing Cone Penetration Test (CPT) data. A substantial CPT database containing 2,516,979 measurement records from various locations in Austria was analyzed to forecast two geotechnical parameters: directly measured shear wave velocity (Vs) from seismic CPT (SCPT) tests and the Robertson [1] Soil Behavior Type index (Ic SBT). The hybrid architecture combines a 3-qubit parameterized quantum circuit that uses angle embedding and strongly entangling layers with classical neural network layers for pre- and post-processing. The whole thing is trained end-to-end was adopted using L-BFGS-B optimization on a PCA-reduced feature space. Five-fol…

item.title: Discovering Reaction Mechanisms with Transition Path Sampling-Based Active Learning of Machine-Learned Potentials
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Ashique Lal, Rik S. Breebaart, Peter G. Bolhuis, Evert Jan Meijer
arXiv:2605.03737v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Machine-learned interatomic potentials (MLPs) provide near density functional theory (DFT) accuracy at reduced computational cost, but their reliability depends on representative training data and often deteriorates in transition-state regions governing rare events. We introduce an active-learning framework in which Transition Path Sampling (TPS) serves as a targeted data-generation engine for constructing MLPs accurate in barrier regions. TPS generates ensembles of unbiased reactive trajectories, and a committee-based uncertainty estimate identifies configurations for selective DFT labeling and retraining. Iterating this cycle systematically refines the potential energy surface in dynamically relevant regions, without the need of prior know…

item.title: 利用大语言模型发现强化学习接口
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Akshat Singh Jaswal, Ashish Baghel, Paras Chopra
该工作研究从原始模拟器状态自动发现强化学习任务接口的问题，即同时生成观测映射和奖励函数。提出LIMEN框架，利用大语言模型引导进化算法生成可执行程序形式的候选接口，并通过策略训练反馈迭代优化。在离散网格世界和连续控制（运动与操作）领域的实验表明，仅给定轨迹级成功指标时，观测与奖励的联合进化能够发现有效接口，而单独优化任一组件至少在一个领域失效。

item.title: [ASAP] Integrating Theory and Experiment with Graph Neural Networks to Classify Molecular Self-Assembly on Metal Surfaces
item.description:
📚 Nano Letters: Latest Articles (ACS Publications)
👤 Linbin Zhu, Shaochen Hu, Xianpeng Wang, Haiming Zhang, Lizhen Huang, Youyong Li, Lu Wang, and Lifeng Chi
Nano Letters DOI: 10.1021/acs.nanolett.6c00359

item.title: Neural-network reconstruction of THz transmission spectra using electrically tunable AlGaN/GaN plasmonic-crystal analyzer
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 A. Witkowska, M. Dub, P. Sai, P. Tiwari, M. Sakowicz, J. A. Majewski, W. Knap
arXiv:2605.03778v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: We demonstrate machine learning (ML) based reconstruction of terahertz transmission spectra using an electrically tunable grating-gate AlGaN/GaN plasmonic-crystal analyzer. The analyzer encodes the transmission spectrum into a voltage-dependent intensity, which is then inverted by an ML algorithm. A feedforward neural network trained on a synthetic dataset is validated experimentally on four samples in standard Fourier Transform Infrared (FTIR) mode and in direct (fixed-mirror) acquisition mode. The network achieves a mean square error (MSE) of the reconstruction of 0.015 in FTIR mode and 0.038 in direct mode, correctly identifying six out of seven ground-truth resonances in each mode. Against a first-difference Tikhonov regularization bas…

item.title: Intelligent Accompaniment Generation and Emotional Adaptation Using Graph Neural Networks to Model Chord Progression Logic
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Suhao Bai
Current automatic accompaniment generation methods based on sequence models struggle to fully capture the long-term functional logic between chords, and their emotional adaptation accuracy is limited. To address this, this paper employs an intelligent accompaniment generation model based on a GNN (Graph Neural Network) and an EC-GNN-AG (Emotion-Conditioned Graph Neural Network for Accompaniment Generation) to explicitly model the functional logic between chords and achieve high-precision emotional control. This method constructs chord sequences as a multi-relational graph structure. The Graph Attention Network (GAT) is used to encode the harmonic functional relationships and surface affinities between nodes, and a cross-modal emotional attention mechanism is designed. Discrete or continuo…

item.title: Random test functions, H⁻¹ norm equivalence, and stochastic variational physics-informed neural networks
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Diego Marcondes
arXiv:2605.03542v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: The dual norm characterisation of weak solutions of second-order linear elliptic partial differential equations is mathematically natural but computationally intractable: evaluating the H⁻¹ norm of a residual requires a supremum over an infinite-dimensional function space. We prove that the H⁻¹ norm of any functional is equivalent to its expected squared evaluation against a random test function whose distribution depends only on the domain. Crucially, realisations of this random test function have negative Sobolev regularity for d ≥ 2, yet this roughness is not an obstacle: averaging over the distribution exactly recovers the correct weak topology, independently of the differential operator. This equivalence introduces the notion of stoch…

item.title: Anderson Localization with Single Photons from a Quantum Emitter
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Simon J. U. White, Diego N. Bernal-García, Toan Trong Tran, Igor Aharonovich, Alexander S. Solntsev
arXiv:2605.03251v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Anderson localization of light is a fundamental emergent phenomenon in disordered systems. In arrays of coupled waveguides, it suppresses transport and causes photons to remain localized near the excitation site as coupling disorder increases. Here, we experimentally demonstrate Anderson localization using single photons emitted by a single-photon emitter in hexagonal boron nitride at room temperature. Despite the limited temporal coherence of the emitter, the photons undergo pronounced Anderson localization, evidenced by exponentially localized output intensity profiles in disordered waveguide lattices. Beyond the experimental demonstration, we develop a general theoretical framework for wave propagation in disordered tight-binding systems,…

item.title: Can phaseless auxiliary-field quantum Monte Carlo with broken symmetry trials describe iron-sulfur clusters?
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Eirik F. Kjønstad, Huanchen Zhai, James Shee, Sandeep Sharma, Garnet Kin-Lic Chan
arXiv:2605.03270v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Phaseless auxiliary-field quantum Monte Carlo (AFQMC) has in several cases been found to perform well on strongly correlated systems. Here, we benchmark the method for three iron-sulfur clusters ([2Fe-2S], [4Fe-4S], and the FeMo cofactor) using a hierarchy of trial states derived from coupled cluster (CC) theory, including up to quadruple excitations, as well as multi-Slater trial states derived from the density matrix renormalization group. Our results reveal for these systems that, as the symmetry-broken trial is improved, the phaseless AFQMC energy can become less accurate, and in some cases even less accurate than the underlying trial projected energy, displaying an inverted energy pattern that is only corrected once the trial fidelity i…

item.title: Coherent transport in non-Abelian quantum graphs
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 A. V. Poshakinskiy, L. E. Golub
arXiv:2605.03826v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study quantum charge transport in two-dimensional networks in the presence of a magnetic field and spin-orbit interaction. The interplay of the corresponding Abelian and non-Abelian gauge fields leads to an intricate behavior of the conductance, which has different periodicities in the diffusive and ballistic regimes. We classify all configurations of magnetic and spin-orbit fields where a logarithmically divergent weak-(anti)localization correction appears in the diffusive regime. The conductivity of topologically distinct configurations is the same in the diffusive regime but different in the ballistic regime. The proposed setup provides a feasible realization of quantum graphs with non-Abelian gauge fields.

item.title: Deterministic positioning of circular Bragg gratings using atomic force lithography for high-performance quantum dot light sources
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Sai Abhishikth Dhurjati, Moritz Langer, Yared G. Zena, Ahmad Rahimi, Liesa Raith, Martin Bauer, Frank H. P. Fitzek, Riccardo Bassoli, Caspar Hopfmann
arXiv:2605.03672v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Semiconductor quantum dots (QDs) grown by molecular beam epitaxy are excellent quantum emitters, but their random spatial distribution hinders deterministic coupling to optical microcavities. We demonstrate a room-temperature atomic force microscopy (AFM)-assisted nano-oxidation lithography technique enabling QD positioning with a radial displacement of 51(28) nm. Free-standing asymmetric circular Bragg gratings incorporating AFM-positioned GaAs QDs exhibit a 245-fold photoluminescence enhancement and fine-structure splitting (FSS) comparable to bulk QDs. Polarization-resolved spectroscopy and finite-difference time-domain simulations show robust emission for displacements up to 50 nm (Stokes parameter łvert S v̊ert < 0.05 ). The devices dis…

item.title: First-Order Transitions in Weak Ising Spin-Orbit-Coupled Superconductors
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Xusheng Wang, Gaomin Tang, Shuai-hua Ji
arXiv:2605.03774v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Ising spin-orbit coupling (ISOC) can strongly protect superconductivity against exchange-field-induced depairing, typically leading to critical fields far exceeding the Pauli limit and continuous (second-order) phase transitions. Here, using a free-energy approach, we demonstrate that first-order transitions can emerge in superconductors with weak ISOC under large exchange fields. In this regime, conventional theoretical approaches based on the gap equation fail to determine the thermodynamic critical field and instead yield only the supercooling field. Moreover, we identify two pronounced in-gap coherence peaks in the quasiparticle spectra, which represent the weak-ISOC manifestation of the previously reported mirage-gap states. Our results…

item.title: Gossamer Superconductivity in Moiré WSe₂ Bilayer
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Hui-Ke Jin, Guangyue Ji, Zhan Wang, Jie Wang, Fu-Chun Zhang
arXiv:2605.03766v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Moiré transition metal dichalcogenides have served as a versatile platform for simulating Hubbard physics. Recent experiments have identified robust superconductivity in moiré bilayer WSe₂ for certain twist angles. Here, we propose the gossamer nature of the superconductivity recently discovered at half-filling and zero displacement field in twisted WSe₂. By mapping the moiré continuum system to an effective extended single-orbital Hubbard model on the triangular lattice, we employ renormalized mean-field theory to investigate the strong-coupling phase diagram. We find that a moderate Coulomb repulsion partially suppresses charge fluctuations while preserving a finite density of mobile doublons and holes. In this regime, the interplay betwee…

item.title: Magneto Transport and Spin Reorientation in Pt Co78Ho22 Heterostructures Near the Sublattice Compensation Temperature
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Rajeev Nepal, Jose Flores, Aurain Seaton, Michael Newburger, John Derek Demaree, Ramesh C Budhani
arXiv:2605.03982v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Metallic amorphous ferrimagnets of 3d transition metals (TM) and rare earths (RE) with 4f electrons exhibit rich magneto transport behavior due to the interplay between the 3d and 4f magnetic sublattices and their interaction with mobile charges. Tuning the TM and RE concentrations in the alloy can effectively modulate the compensation temperature, where the moments of the two sublattices point in opposite direction leading to a net zero magnetization. Despite extensive magnetotransport studies in Gd and Tb based 3d 4f systems, Ho based alloys remain comparatively underexplored, even though Ho possesses the largest orbital angular momentum (OAM) among the lanthanides. This unquenched OAM can strongly impact magnetic anisotropy and magnetotra…

item.title: Nonuniform superconducting states from Majorana flat bands
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Sushanth Varada, Aksel Kobiałka, Ankita Bhattacharya, Patric Holmvall, Annica M. Black-Schaffer
arXiv:2605.03859v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Zero-energy flat bands within the superconducting gap can give rise to competing ordered phases. We investigate such phases in topological superconductors based on the magnetic adatom platform hosting a flat band of Majorana edge states. Our self-consistent calculations of the superconducting order parameter show the emergence of both a pair density wave with edge-localized amplitude modulations and a phase crystal characterized by edge-localized phase modulations. These two phases lower the free energy of the system by gapping out the Majorana flat band, as dictated by winding numbers, which are primarily tuned by the chemical potential. In fact, at zero temperature the uniform superconducting solution with Majorana flat band never survives…

item.title: Quantum Metric Localization and Quantum Metric Protection
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Wen-Bo Dai, Jinchao Zhao, Shuai A. Chen, K. T. Law
arXiv:2605.03987v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The study of disorder effects in electronic systems is one of the central themes in physics. It is well established that in the Anderson localization regime, the localization length of electrons decreases monotonically as the disorder strength increases. Here, we demonstrate that the conventional Anderson localization paradigm fails completely in describing an isolated band with quantum metric, where the quantum metric of the band defines a length scale called the quantum metric length. For an isolated band with a finite bandwidth separated from other bands by a band gap Δ, weak disorder results in conventional Anderson localization behavior. However, as the disorder increases, the localization length ceases to decrease and becomes pinned at…

item.title: Remote entropy measurement in coupled quantum dots
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Owen Sheekey, Tim Child, Elena Cornick, Saeed Fallahi, Geoffrey C. Gardner, Michael J. Manfra, Eran Sela, Yaakov Kleeorin, Yigal Meir, Silvia Lüscher, Joshua Folk
arXiv:2605.03876v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent experiments have demonstrated that measurements of the entropy change associated with the addition of electrons to semiconductor- and graphene-based quantum dots accurately quantify the spin and orbital degeneracy of the states into which they are added. However, measuring more exotic entropies requires probing the entropy change of an entire system in response to an added particle. Here, we demonstrate that Maxwell relation-based measurements probe not only the entropy change associated with the added electron but also that of the surrounding system as it responds to that electron. Using a pair of capacitively coupled GaAs quantum dots, we show that charge measurements on one dot reveal entropy changes associated with the entire two-…

item.title: Spontaneous Topological Locking and Symmetry Restoration of Meron Lattices in Synthetic Antiferromagnets
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Gülşen Doğan, Ümit Akinci
arXiv:2605.03755v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Synthetic antiferromagnets offer a robust platform for stabilizing fractional topological textures, effectively circumventing the limitations of ferromagnetic systems. In this study, we utilize large-scale Monte Carlo simulations to investigate the spontaneous topological locking and structural symmetry restoration of meron-antimeron crystals within SAF bilayers subjected to easy-plane magnetic anisotropy. In the uncoupled monolayer limit, increasing anisotropy induces an extreme core-shrinking effect that physically expands the inter-core distance and triggers a C₄ i̊ghtarrow C₂ symmetry breaking. However, the introduction of an ultra-weak interlayer antiferromagnetic exchange acts as an active structural scaffold. For rigid crystals, this…

item.title: 量子晶格系统的Berry-Esseen界
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Marcus Cramer, Fernando G. S. L. Brandão, Mădălin Guţă, Álvaro M. Alhambra, Matteo Scandi
该研究证明了量子晶格系统的Berry-Esseen定理版本，为量子系统的中心极限定理提供了严格的有限尺寸收敛估计。对于N粒子上的局域量子哈密顿量和具有有限关联长度的量子态，测量局域可观测量（如能量）的结果服从正态分布，误差以O(N⁻¹/²polylog(N))标度，该标度在对数因子意义下最优。

item.title: 用于百万量级磁位形探索的可编程仿星器-托卡马克混合装置
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Gudong Yu, Xianyi Nie, Gwanggeun Seo, Daxing Huang, Hengqian Liu, Junhao Liu, Jaebeom Cho, Hyun-Su Kim, Jinlin Xie, Ge Zhuang, Fazhu Ding, Jong-Kyu Park, Caoxiang Zhu
该工作提出一种可编程的仿星器-托卡马克混合装置，利用一组固定平面线圈接入广泛的磁位形空间。该装置在类托卡马克线圈组基础上增加288个偶极场线圈，仅通过对称性要求六种独立线圈几何结构。通过编程线圈电流，同一套硬件可生成超过166万种优化的仿星器位形，涵盖准轴对称、准螺旋对称和准等动态性，以及托卡马克相关的三维扰动。代表性位形表现出嵌套磁面、低新经典输运和良好的高能粒子约束特性。

item.title: 受挫自旋纹理诱导三重态超导体d矢量的各向异性约瑟夫森耦合
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Grayson R. Frazier, Junyi Zhang, Yi Li
该工作证明巡游电子与非共线经典交换场耦合可在三重态超导体的d矢量间诱导各向异性约瑟夫森耦合，类似于磁性中的Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya和Γ型相互作用。基于几何阻挫晶格上的s-d模型微扰分析，非共线局域自旋纹理产生自旋三重态配对关联，并通过d矢量间的各向异性约瑟夫森耦合 favors 空间变化的超导序，赋予配对序一种与超流刚度竞争的"可塑性"。对于非幺正配对，d矢量的空间纹理可在无外磁场时产生反常涡旋；作者还预测了与底层磁纹理自旋手性成正比的约瑟夫森二极管效应。

item.title: Behavior-dLDS：行为部分约束的神经活动分解线性动态系统模型
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Eva Yezerets, En Yang, Misha B. Ahrens, Adam S. Charles
该工作提出行为分解线性动态系统（b-dLDS），用于解耦大规模神经群体记录中与行为直接相关的网络与并行运行的内部计算。基于行为是神经活动的粗粒度低维潜动态产物的观点，b-dLDS能够同时识别行为生成子系统与内部计算子系统，并在模拟数据中验证了其相对于现有方法的改进，为理解脑-行为关系的并行分布式特性提供了新建模工具。

item.title: Cr₃₊δTe₄中竞争磁相的空间分离
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 V. K. Bhartiya, Anirban Goswami, Nicholas Ng, Wei Tian, Matthew G. Tucker, Niraj Aryal, Lijun Wu, Weiguo Yin, Yimei Zhu, Milinda Abeykoon, Emmanuel Yakubu, Samaresh Guchhait, J. M. Tranquada
该研究通过中子衍射发现，单晶Cr₃₊δTe₄（δ=-0.10）存在两种单斜相：一种在约321 K以下呈现铁磁有序，另一种在约86 K以下出现反铁磁有序；而δ=-0.26的晶体仅表现铁磁序。结合中子粉末衍射、X射线粉末衍射和透射电镜分析，作者推断两相以小于100 nm的细晶互生长方式共存，可能源于高温spinodal分解。理论计算支持铁磁相与反铁磁相的晶格结构弛豫差异。

item.title: 基于广义矩阵乘积拟设构造精确量子多体疤痕态
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Sascha Gehrmann, Fabian H. L. Essler
该研究基于受非对称简单排他过程稳态求解方法启发的局域误差抵消拟设，为非 frustration-free 哈密顿量的量子多体系统构造了矩阵乘积形式的精确本征态。该方法在一维和二维空间维度中均有显式示例验证。

item.title: 电磁场下物质规范场介导的对称性破缺及其对自旋动力学的影响
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Uiseok Jeong, Esmaeil Taghizadeh Sisakht, Angel Rubio, Carsten A. Ullrich, Kyeong-Whan Kim, Noejung Park
该研究表明，在外加电磁场中，自旋-轨道耦合(SOC)的规范不变表述中的规范场项对对称性破缺和自旋动力学起主导作用，而非通常认为可忽略的附加项。作者通过实时含时密度泛函理论计算，展示了规范场项扰动镜像、滑移和螺旋旋转对称性时，动态自旋态如何随时间演化超越对称冻结态。对于非平衡自旋-轨道动力学，规范不变表述不仅在形式上必需，在定量上也至关重要。

item.title: Identifying Instabilities with Quantum Geometry in Flat Band Systems
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Jia-Xin Zhang, Wen O. Wang, Leon Balents, Lucile Savary
arXiv:2504.03882v3 Announce Type: replace Abstract: The absence of a well-defined Fermi surface in flat-band systems challenges the conventional understanding of instabilities toward Landau order based on nesting. We investigate the existence of an intrinsic nesting structure encoded in the band geometry (i.e. the wavefunctions of the flat band(s)), which leads to a maximal susceptibility at the mean-field level and thus determines the instability towards ordered phases. More generally, we show that for a given band structure and observable, we can define two vector fields: one which corresponds to the Bloch vector of the projection operator onto the manifold of flat bands, and another which is "dressed" by the observable. The overlap between the two vector fields, possibly shifted by a m…

item.title: 量子电池中非稳定资源与可用功的 interplay
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Tanoy Kanti Konar, Jakub Zakrzewski
该研究探究了非稳定资源（nonstabilizerness）在量子电池中的作用，考虑N个自旋-1/2粒子系统，左半作为充电器、右半作为电池。通过研究充电器与电池间的不同相互作用类别，量化了存储功所需的非稳定资源量。结果表明，当相互作用哈密顿量保持U(1)对称性时，电池中存储的可用功（ergotropy）与复合系统的总非稳定资源之间存在一一对应关系；而对于不守恒该对称性的更一般相互作用，这种对应关系通常丧失。此外，当初始化为非稳定态的电池通过Clifford演化充电时，最大平均充电功率对初始非稳定资源呈非单调依赖，最高功率出现在有限非稳定资源处。

item.title: 动力学约束腔量子电动力学：从阻塞铁磁性到长程量子疤痕
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Hossein Hosseinabadi, Riccardo J. Valencia-Tortora, Aleksandr N. Mikheev, Darrick E. Chang, Johannes Zeiher, Roderich Moessner, Jamir Marino
该工作提出一个可扩展的Rydberg-腔混合系统最小模型，在强Rydberg阻塞极限下将希尔伯特空间约束至阻塞子空间，得到一维长程动力学约束模型。平衡态下除顺磁和Néel相外，系统还支持区别于常规超辐射相的阻塞铁磁/超辐射相。该模型既捕捉强Rydberg相互作用区域的实验物理，又为研究长程与短程相互作用竞争提供透明理论框架，并揭示非平衡动力学中的长程量子疤痕现象。

item.title: 立方PrIr₂Zn₂₀中磁场下L点四极序
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Hitoko Okanoya, Kazumasa Hattori
该工作基于朗道理论研究重费米子化合物PrIr₂Zn₂₀在磁场下的四极序。假设面心立方晶格布里渊区L点处存在实验观测到的E_g电四极序，作者构建朗道自由能并分析发现，此前报道的磁场H∥[001]下未识别的高温有序相源于Pr位f电子四极矩的旋转。该工作还讨论了其他磁场方向的相图及可能的双q四极有序。

item.title: Magnetically Programmable Surface Acoustic Wave Filters: Device Concept and Predictive Modeling
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Michael K. Steinbauer, Peter Flauger, Matthias Küß, Stephan Glamsch, Emeline D. S. Nysten, Matthias Weiß, Dieter Suess, Hubert J. Krenner, Manfred Albrecht, Claas Abert
arXiv:2507.23456v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Filtering surface acoustic wave (SAW) signals of specified frequencies depending on the strength of an external magnetic field in a magnetostrictive material has garnered significant interest due to its potential scientific and industrial applications. Here, we propose a device that achieves selective SAW attenuation by instead programming its internal magnetic state. To this end, we perform micromagnetic simulations for the magnetoelastic interaction of the Rayleigh SAW mode with spin waves (SWs) in exchange-decoupled Co/Ni islets on a piezoelectric LiTaO₃ substrate. Due to the islets exhibiting perpendicular magnetic anisotropy, the stray-field interaction between them leads to a shift in the SW dispersion depending on the magnetic ali…

item.title: Mapping reservoir-enhanced superconductivity to near-long-range magnetic order in the undoped one-dimensional Anderson and Kondo lattices
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 J. E. Ebot, Lorenzo Pizzino, Sam Mardazad, Johannes S. Hofmann, Thierry Giamarchi, Adrian Kantian
arXiv:2602.11153v3 Announce Type: replace Abstract: The undoped Kondo necklace in 1D is a paradigmatic and well understood model of a Kondo insulator. This work performs the first large-scale study of the 1D Anderson-lattice underlying the Kondo necklace with quasi-exact numerical methods, comparing this with the perturbative effective 1D Kondo-necklace model derived from the former. This study is based on an exact mapping of the Anderson model to one of a superconducting pairing layer connected to a metallic reservoir which is valid in arbitrary spatial dimensions, thereby linking the previously disparate areas of reservoir-enhanced superconductivity, following Kivelson's pioneering proposals, and that of periodic Kondo-systems. Our work reveals that below the length-scales on which the…

item.title: 基于单轴各向异性自旋-1 XXZ模型的测量型量子计算
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Hiroki Ohta, Aaron Merlin Müller, Shunji Tsuchiya
该研究证明具有单离子各向异性D和Ising型各向异性J的自旋-1 XXZ链在Haldane相的基态可作为测量型量子计算的资源态，实现单量子比特门操作。当D或J适当调节时，基本旋转门及一般单比特幺正门的保真度超过0.99。作者在ℤ₂×ℤ₂保护Haldane相假设下导出旋转门保真度的解析表达式，表明其由测量后自旋-自旋关联函数与失败概率决定。保真度的提升源于反铁磁相附近AFM关联的增强，有效抑制了失败态。

item.title: 非线性介电材料中电磁场的路径积分量子化
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Arman Kashef, Oscar Perearnau Herrero, Alexander Szameit, Marco Ornigotti, Stefan Scheel
该工作构建了含色散与吸收的非线性介电介质中光的量子理论，采用包含四阶非线性的介观光-物质相互作用模型。通过路径积分形式积分掉物质与热浴自由度得到有效作用量，导出Kerr非线性响应函数，并完成全场量子化，推导出该理论的费曼规则。

item.title: 三角PtBi₂中点接触增强超导性：高Tc起源的探索
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 O. E. Kvitnitskaya, L. Harnagea, G. Shipunov, S. Aswartham, I. Kovalchuk, V. V. Fisun, D. V. Efremov, B. Büchner, Yu. G. Naidyuk
该工作研究I型外尔半金属三角相t-PtBi₂点接触（PC）中的增强超导性，使用正常金属（Ag、Cu、Pt）和铁磁（Fe、Co、Ni）针尖测量微分电阻dV/dI(V)曲线。多数情况下超导临界温度Tc在3-5 K之间，数倍于体相最高Tc；少数PC的Tc高达8 K，且正常金属与铁磁针尖均可实现。临界磁场也显著增强至数特斯拉。Tc升高的共同原因可能与PC形成过程中的压力/应变有关，样品薄片边缘形成的PC比平面处Tc提升更大。铁磁针尖兼容Tc增强，暗示增强超导性可能具有复杂本质。

item.title: 静磁场与旋转磁场中自旋-J粒子的量子动力学：纠缠共振与扭结
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Nargis Sultana, Siddharth Seetharaman, Rejish Nath
该研究分析了单自旋-J及双自旋-J粒子在静磁场与旋转磁场中的量子动力学，发现两类最大拉伸态之间存在与J值无关的共振周期性振荡，以及磁量子数相反子能级间的周期性跃迁。对于双自旋系统，推导了多种共振条件并分析偶极-偶极相互作用产生的纠缠；双自旋-1/2情形中，最大纠缠呈现共振与扭结特征，其判据可由能谱获得，且扭结可被利用以调控纠缠动力学。弱偶极相互作用区域与偶极玻色-爱因斯坦凝聚体相关。

item.title: 混合态的量子演化与量子热机性能
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Anatoly A. Svidzinsky, Wenzhuo Zhang
该工作引入沿海森堡绘景计算密度算符时间演化的技术，求得初始处于不同温度热态的两耦合与三耦合谐振子量子演化的精确解，揭示振子因能量交换诱导关联而交换热态、产生噪声诱导相干的有趣动力学。将该框架应用于含时频率耦合光学腔光子量子热机，证明参数共振条件下末态激子数交换时做功最大，且Carnot公式在一般假设下给出QHE极限效率。

item.title: 量子度规对刘维尔定理与手征动理学理论的修正
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Kazuya Mameda, Naoki Yamamoto
该研究基于狄拉克括号建立包含动量空间量子度规的准粒子正则形式体系，证明量子度规在ℏ²阶修正相空间态密度，从而导致刘维尔定理、动理学理论及相关物理量的修正。在非均匀电场中，量子度规诱导能量密度与能流的修正。应用于手征费米子，该框架给出了与量子场论一致的手征动理学理论非线性推广，为量子度规在高能物理和天体物理中的应用开辟道路。

item.title: 局域非中心对称超导体涡旋态的准经典理论：应用于CeRh₂As₂
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Akihiro Minamide, Youichi Yanase
该工作采用准经典方法研究具有局域反演对称性破缺的双层超导体的涡旋态，以解释2021年发现的重费米子超导体CeRh₂As₂在c轴磁场下呈现两个不同超导相的非常规相图。计算涡旋晶格态的局域态密度（LDOS）发现，不同超导态的配对对称性在涡旋芯部LDOS峰结构中清晰显现；由于LDOS可实验观测，该工作为CeRh₂As₂中超导宇称转变的实验验证提供了途径。

item.title: 垂直磁隧道结中的射频辅助翻转
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Mark Hayward, Salvatore Perna, Massimiliano d'Aquino, Claudio Serpico, Wonjoon Jung, Chunhui Dai, Patrick M. Braganca, Ilya N. Krivorotov
该工作首次研究了射频自旋矩辅助的垂直STT-MRAM写入方案。实验表明，在直流写入脉冲前施加小幅度射频脉冲可提升磁隧道结的翻转概率，且低频射频的效率增益更高。该RF+DC方案允许缩短直流脉冲宽度，从而改善器件耐久性。解析模型与数值模拟定性复现了实验趋势，但定量差异表明实际MTJ特性超出理想化模型，在射频辅助翻转中起重要作用。

item.title: 量子网络中的鲁棒自旋压缩：普适性的启示
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Andrea Solfanelli, Augusto Smerzi, Peter Zoller, Nicolò Defenu
该研究建立了在任意非均匀网络几何中嵌入的相互作用自旋系综实现可扩展自旋压缩的条件。研究识别了两种压缩形式：类OAT可扩展压缩仅由相互作用图的普适性质（由其谱维度控制）决定；而临界压缩中谱维度仅提供可扩展计量增益的必要条件，充分条件要求模型处于对称性破缺转变之下。因此，量子网络中自旋压缩临界点的标度源于xy铁磁普适性与逾渗普适性之间的非平凡 interplay。该框架为设计跨平台的可扩展量子传感器提供了统一视角。

item.title: Selecting optimal unrestricted Hartree-Fock trial wavefunctions for phaseless auxiliary-field quantum Monte Carlo: Accuracy and limitations in modeling three iron-sulfur clusters
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Don Danilov, Brad Ganoe, Leon Otis, Zhi Gong, Zixiang Lu, James Shee
arXiv:2605.03981v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Phaseless auxiliary-field quantum Monte Carlo (ph-AFQMC) has emerged as a promising electronic structure method for correlated electronic systems. However, the quality of its predictions depends critically on the choice of trial wavefunction, and it is not obvious how to make an optimal choice especially for strongly correlated states of large systems. Mean-field wavefunctions are compelling trial wavefunction candidates as they map directly to chemical concepts and can be obtained with O(N⁴⁾ cost. Yet in the strongly correlated regime one faces a symmetry dilemma and the existence of multiple nearly-degenerate solutions. In this work we investigate active space models of [2Fe-2S]²⁺, mixed-valent [4Fe-4S]²⁺, and [4Fe-4S]⁴⁺ and explore the…

item.title: 受挫自旋纹理诱导的空间非均匀三重态配对序与约瑟夫森二极管效应
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Grayson R. Frazier, Yi Li
该工作证明受挫自旋纹理可在三重态超导体d矢量间产生各向异性约瑟夫森耦合，稳定空间变化的配对序。这些耦合依赖于d矢量相对取向，类似于磁性中的Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya和Γ型相互作用，导致与超流刚度竞争的配对序"可塑性"。此类耦合非自旋-轨道耦合起源，而可源于巡游电子与受挫自旋矩构成的局域交换场耦合。利用T矩阵展开，作者证明与局域交换场耦合导致依赖于超导颗粒间势垒自旋构型的有效巡游电子隧穿；通过受挫自旋纹理的约瑟夫森隧穿可产生约瑟夫森二极管效应，其源于势垒中的非零自旋手性或非共线d矢量间的反对称约瑟夫森耦合，二者均破缺反演对称性。

item.title: 三角形网络中的实验真量子非定域性
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Lett.
👤 Ning-Ning Wang, Chao Zhang, Huan Cao, Kai Xu, Bi-Heng Liu, Yun-Feng Huang, Chuan-Feng Li, Guang-Can Guo, Nicolas Gisin, Tamás Kriváchy, and Marc-Olivier Renou
近十年来研究表明，涉及多个独立纠缠源、由多方分发和测量的量子网络在执行纠缠测量时，可产生全新的非经典量子关联形式。该工作在三角形网络中实验实现了真正的量子非定域性，详情需查阅原文。

item.title: Neural spectroscopy reveals structure from a learned vacuum
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Daeho Park
Excited states encode the mechanisms of interacting quantum systems and the structure of the underlying theory. But in first-principles calculations they are generally much harder to extract and interpret reliably than ground states. Neural-network wavefunctions have expanded access to ground states, yet excited-state extensions typically remain state-specific, optimization-intensive and judged mainly by benchmark energies. This paper shows, in a proof-of-principle study of compact U(1) gauge theory in two spatial dimensions, that a high-quality gauge-equivariant architecture trained only on the ground state can be reused as a generator of structure-resolved spectral data with microscopic details. Combined with correlation-matrix variational analysis, a single learned vacuum yields an exc…

item.title: 化学反应网络中熵产生的普适临界性
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Kyota Tamano, Keiji Saito
该工作研究宏观非平衡转变处熵产生的临界行为。在宏观优先极限下，利用线性噪声公式、中心流形正规形和Floquet理论，获得了叉式、跨临界、鞍结和Hopf分岔处熵产生涨落与响应的普适指数。轨迹空间的Cramér-Rao型界限给出普适标度不等式α-2β≥0，表明发散响应需要发散涨落但反之不然，使熵产生涨落成为非平衡临界性的更敏锐探针。

item.title: Impact of Mineral Interface Network Topology on the Micromechanical Properties of Central Quartz Grains in Shale: Rigid Constraint and Compliant Decoupling
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 YUNFEI GUO, Hucheng Deng, Jianhua He, Ruixue Li
The accurate prediction of shale's macroscopic mechanical behavior has long been limited by insufficient understanding of its micro-scale heterogeneity. Traditional rock physics models often overlook micro-scale heterogeneity by assuming constant mineral properties, failing to explain the significant mechanical variability observed in experiments. To overcome the limitations of component-based analysis, we integrated high-resolution SEM-EDS with targeted nanoindentation to investigate how interface network topology controls the micromechanical response of central quartz grains in the Lower Cambrian Qiongzhusi Formation shale (Sichuan Basin, China). A "weighted interface constraint strength coefficient" (Ceff) was proposed to quantify the local micromechanical environment. Results indicate…

item.title: Linear and Non-Linear Rheology of Single and Double Cross-Linked Biopolymer Networks under Viscous Shear Flow
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Nasrollah Hajaliakbari, David Head, Oliver Harlen
arXiv:2605.03728v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In this research study, a numerical tool, which is based on a version of Slender Body theory, has been used and also modified to simulate the mechanical behaviour of single- and double-cross-linked biopolymer networks (hydrogel) under oscillatory shear flow. The hydrodynamic interactions among fibres of intertwined networks were considered. Then, the stress and Fourier coefficients (i.e. shear moduli) were evaluated for both linear and nonlinear regimes. It was found that the double peaks (two-step yielding) of two double network at 100% maximum strain amplitude (nonlinear regime) cannot happen due to changes in fibre alignments and seed numbers, although the crosslinkers between two subnetworks present, which was previously reported in the…

item.title: Posterior-First Neural PDE Simulation: Inferring Hidden Problem State from a Single Field
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Wenshuo Wang, Fan Zhang
arXiv:2605.03247v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Neural PDE simulators often receive only a single observed field at deployment. In this setting, a field-to-future predictor can collapse distinct latent problem states into the same deterministic interface, losing the ambiguity needed for reliable rollout and downstream decisions. We propose posterior-first neural PDE simulation: first infer a posterior over the minimal task-sufficient problem state, then condition prediction on that posterior. The resulting theory connects the object, the learning target, and the failure mode: Bayes downstream values factor through this posterior, refinement labels make it learnable by proper scoring rules, and deterministic collapse incurs an ambiguity barrier whenever the true posterior is non-Dirac. Syn…

item.title: 从多尺度细节层次特征学习判别性符号距离函数用于三维异常检测
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Haibo Xiao, Hanzhe Liang, Jie Zhou, Jinbao Wang, Can Gao
该工作提出一种基于表面的三维异常检测方法，利用多尺度细节层次特征学习判别性符号距离函数。方法包含三个核心模块：噪声点生成（NPG）模块通过生成不同类型噪声暴露异常点以促进判别特征学习；多尺度细节层次特征（MLF）模块捕捉点云的多尺度信息，提供细粒度局部和粗粒度全局特征；隐式表面判别（ISD）模块利用提取的多尺度特征学习点云的隐式表面表示，有效训练符号距离函数。

item.title: Energy-Efficient Routing Protocols in IoT Networks: A Narrative Review and Comparative Simulation Study
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Mohammad Haribat, Ahmad Kayed
The battery life of IoT sensors is often the limiting factor, and the choice of routing protocol directly affects how quickly nodes deplete their power. Although many protocols have been proposed, published results use disparate configurations and cannot be compared directly. This paper accomplishes two objectives. First, we give a narrative review of six protocol families: hierarchical, flat, location-based, multipath, QoS- or standard-based, and learning-based. Second, we use the first-order radio energy model of Heinzelman et al. to build an open-source Python/NumPy simulator and run a side-by-side comparison of six reference protocols: FLAT, LEACH, EWC (inspired by HEED), PEGASIS, SSPT (inspired by RPL), and QL-F (a minimal baseline Q-learning implementation included for comparison on…

item.title: Local Truncation Error-Guided Neural ODEs for Large Scale Traffic Forecasting
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Xiao Zhang, Yafei Li, Ruixiang Wang, Wei Wei, Shuo He, Mingliang Xu
arXiv:2605.03386v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Spatiotemporal forecasting in physical systems, such as large-scale traffic networks, requires modeling a dual dynamic: continuous macroscopic rhythms and discrete, unpredictable microscopic shocks. While Neural Ordinary Differential Equations (ODEs) excel at capturing smooth evolution, their inherent Lipschitz continuity constraints inevitably cause severe over-smoothing when confronting abrupt anomalies. Recent physics-informed methods attempt to bypass this by penalizing numerical integration errors to enforce manifold smoothness. However, we mathematically reveal that such rigid regularization inherently triggers gradient conflicts and ``attention collapse,'' stripping the model of its sensitivity to anomalies. To resolve this continuity…

item.title: Scaling Laws and Symmetry, Evidence from Neural Force Fields
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Khang Ngo, Siamak Ravanbakhsh
arXiv:2510.09768v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: We present an empirical study in the geometric task of learning interatomic potentials, which shows equivariance matters even more at larger scales; we show a clear power-law scaling behaviour with respect to data, parameters and compute with ``architecture-dependent exponents''. In particular, we observe that equivariant architectures, which leverage task symmetry, scale better than non-equivariant models. Moreover, among equivariant architectures, higher-order representations translate to better scaling exponents. Our analysis also suggests that for compute-optimal training, the data and model sizes should scale in tandem regardless of the architecture. At a high level, these results suggest that, contrary to common belief, we sh…
